sections.paragraph: ϑηρίον, ου, τό [dim. of ϑήρ ‘beast of prey’] beast, (wild) animal of non-human entities Mk 1:13; Ac 11:6; 28:4f (a reptile); Hb 12:20; Js 3:7; Rv 6:8. Of bizarre composite beasts 13:1 and oft. in Rv.—In biting imagery, of people viewed outside the boundaries of civilized society Tit 1:12.
